name: agent-sdlc
description: A full-SDLC pipeline for AI coding agents: idea -> acceptance criteria -> architecture -> techstack -> atomic task plan -> readiness gate -> test-first subagent-driven build -> reviewed PR. Run the whole chain or invoke any stage standalone, resolving its input from any source (prompt, doc, Linear, repo) without weakening the gate or traceability. A dependency-free, hardened enforcement-spine checker (sdlc-check, bare node) verifies the pipeline's mechanically-decidable promises — trace integrity, bidirectional coverage (reviewer-checked criteria still need a carrying task, with a sharpened coverage-forward hint), symmetric dangling-component detection, anchored component resolution, per-AC verification-type parsing, recorded-commit ledger/git correspondence (existence + reachability + subject scope), green-bar evidence, and the terminal AC->proof map at ship — never throwing on ragged input; gate/build/ship invoke it fail-closed with an announced degraded fallback, and its grammar is documented in the stage skills. The build conductor pins its subagent roster, carries a subagent-death policy, diagnoses baseline green-bar runnability, captures harness-run per-task green-bar evidence, and can amend the plan mid-build through the plan method + an inline gate; techstack gates load-bearing library claims with a runnable probe (verified-by-probe / asserted) backed by a time-boxed throwaway spike, and an in-stack fast-path declares 'no new products' at once. A light authoring tier compresses small changes (same gate, build, and checker), per-skill trigger-scope qualifiers keep the auto-triggers from firing on everyday words, and structured 'outside the checker' declarations make non-numbered external components resolvable. Plus three documentation skills: the front-door README, the repo essentials (install, usage, running locally, contributing + community-health files), and full technical internals (architecture rationale, data models, invariants, a complete module/API reference under a coverage-ledger contract).
